query: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status latest news June July 2026, algae paint peeling, is the pool fully repaired, lawsuit status
answer:
I've completed the research on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status. Here's the summary of my findings:

## Key Findings (as of July 3–4, 2026)

**Is the pool fully repaired? NO.** Water was restored (~June 29) for the July 4 America 250 celebrations, but NPS has publicly committed to **draining the pool AGAIN immediately after Independence Day weekend** for a second round of "permanent" repairs to the peeling liner.

**Algae & Paint Peeling:**
- The "American flag blue" **polyurea liner** (materials from Rhino Linings, a truck-bed coating firm) began **peeling and floating to the surface within weeks** of the June installation.
- A persistent **algae bloom** turned water green for over a week; it has "largely cleared" recently after ozone nanobubblers and hydrogen peroxide treatment.

**Costs:** Project has ballooned from Trump's original ~$1–2M/one-week promise to **over $16 million total** (coating contract alone: $6.9M → $14.7M no-bid; plus $1.7M nanobubbler system).

**Vandalism claims:** Trump blamed "radical left vandals" and a "350-foot gash." NPS court filings say sealant was cut with a knife/razor and ~70 fence-post tops thrown in. **7 arrests, 7 citations, 18 police reports** as of June 26. Independent experts and fact-checkers cast doubt, noting heavy equipment/motorcade over the fresh coating may have caused failure, and the reported cut may predate the refill.

**Lawsuit status: ONGOING.** The Cultural Landscape Foundation sued NPS, DOI, and Secretary Burgum on May 11, 2026, alleging **National Historic Preservation Act** violations. On June 23, TCLF asked the judge to block further renovations; the government's June 24–25 filing (with vandalism details) was part of this suit. No final ruling reported. Congressional Democrats are also investigating the no-bid contracts.

Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Reflecting Pool liner was cut with a sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
sources.snippet: NPS court filing: caulk over foam sealant cut with sharp knife/razor; plans to drain pool after July 4 for repairs; 7 arrests, 7 citations, 18 reports; $16M project.

sources.title: A new plan to keep the Reflecting Pool clean - including annual purge of 'nasty' muck
sources.snippet: DOI soliciting bids (due July 8) for long-term maintenance; pool to be drained again after July 4; cost exceeds $14M; annual Feb/March draining.

sources.title: Firm that worked on past Reflecting Pool renovation passed on Trump project after deeming it 'unfeasible'
sources.snippet: Sika declined the job; Rhino Linings supplied polyurea; cost rose to $14.7M; peeling within a month; DOJ filing cites knife/razor cuts.

sources.title: Nonprofit sues the federal government over plans to paint Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool blue
sources.snippet: TCLF sued NPS, DOI, Secretary Burgum May 11, 2026 over National Historic Preservation Act; no-bid contract to Atlantic Industrial Coatings.

sources.title: 'Permanent repair' to Reflecting Pool to come around July 4, Trump says
sources.snippet: Trump said pool will be drained again for 'permanent repair' around July 4; six people arrested for alleged vandalism.

sources.title: Reflecting pool to be drained after Independence Day due to vandalism
sources.snippet: Court document: NPS plans to drain the pool again after Independence Day for repairs after alleged vandalism.
